request: Research app-level no-root interception/modification of Google Maps SDK HTTP requests in a re-signed Android APK, including OkHttp, HttpURLConnection, prior art, Morphe patterns, and runtime signature parsing.

transport.scope: Determine Google Maps SDK Android network stack, headers, and public extensibility APIs from authoritative sources and code.

questions.text: Research Google Maps SDK Android transport internals: OkHttp vs HttpURLConnection/native/GMS process, X-Android-Cert generation, and public APIs for custom auth headers. Provide sourced findings and URLs.

config.question: Research Google Maps SDK Android transport internals: OkHttp vs HttpURLConnection/native/GMS process, X-Android-Cert generation, and public APIs for custom auth headers. Provide sourced findings and URLs.
config.context: Focus on current and historical Maps SDK behavior; distinguish in-app SDK code from Google Play services and renderer processes.

hooking.scope: Assess app-level global OkHttp and HttpURLConnection interception techniques and initialization hooks.

questions.text: Research no-root app-level global HTTP interception: OkHttp interceptors/EventListener, HttpURLConnection URLStreamHandlerFactory, AppComponentFactory/Application hooks, limitations on third-party clients, and concrete code patterns. Provide sources.

config.question: Research no-root app-level global HTTP interception: OkHttp interceptors/EventListener, HttpURLConnection URLStreamHandlerFactory, AppComponentFactory/Application hooks, limitations on third-party clients, and concrete code patterns. Provide sources.
config.context: Determine whether an injected APK patch can intercept requests created by Google Maps SDK or Google Play services.

prior-art.scope: Find ReVanced, LSPosed, Xposed, signature spoofing, Maps API bypass, and X-Android-Cert rewrite prior art.

questions.text: Search GitHub/web for prior art: ReVanced/LSPosed/Xposed signature spoofing for Maps, X-Android-Cert rewrites, intercepted Maps SDK calls, and re-signed APK fixes. Report concrete repositories/files and distinguish root/framework hooks from app-only patches.

config.question: Search GitHub/web for prior art: ReVanced/LSPosed/Xposed signature spoofing for Maps, X-Android-Cert rewrites, intercepted Maps SDK calls, and re-signed APK fixes. Report concrete repositories/files and distinguish root/framework hooks from app-only patches.
config.context: Prioritize code-level evidence and exact mechanisms.

signature.scope: Research runtime APK signing certificate extraction, getPackageArchiveInfo semantics, and signing block parsing.

questions.text: Research PackageManager.getPackageArchiveInfo(GET_SIGNATURES/GET_SIGNING_CERTIFICATES), reading APK signing blocks at runtime, and whether an installed/re-signed app can recover the original certificate from itself. Include code/library options and limitations with sources.

config.question: Research PackageManager.getPackageArchiveInfo(GET_SIGNATURES/GET_SIGNING_CERTIFICATES), reading APK signing blocks at runtime, and whether an installed/re-signed app can recover the original certificate from itself. Include code/library options and limitations with sources.
config.context: Clarify that re-signing replaces signing metadata and assess embedding or extracting the original cert.

morphe.scope: Inspect this repository and formulate realistic Morphe patch insertion strategies based on existing architecture.

questions.text: Inspect the Morph repository read-only. Identify relevant existing Google Maps/signature patches, injection capabilities, app initialization hook points, and concrete Morphe-compatible patterns to implement the most viable approach. Include file:line references and risks.

config.question: Inspect the Morph repository read-only. Identify relevant existing Google Maps/signature patches, injection capabilities, app initialization hook points, and concrete Morphe-compatible patterns to implement the most viable approach. Include file:line references and risks.
config.context: Respect project gotchas: version-agnostic fingerprints and no per-method fingerprint cache misuse.
